methods.description: Collection and cataloging of samples from different outcrops for geochemical analysis.

steps.description: 23 samples were collected, all of them photographed and cataloged before the previous treatment for the geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect, photograph, and catalog samples for geochemical analysis

steps.description: Samples were collected at three different points in the Southern Compartment of the basin.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from specified locations in the basin

methods.description: Analytical procedures for determining total organic carbon, sulfur, and other geochemical components.

steps.description: Weighing of 1 g of the samples ground and sieved at 80 mesh, with subsequent acid attack (HCl), for the removal of the carbonate fraction (decarbonation).
steps.searchable_summary: weigh, grind, sieve, and decarbonate samples using HCl

steps.description: Samples were washed with hot distilled water and oven dried at approximately 80 °C until constant weight was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry samples to constant weight

steps.description: Decarbonated samples were inserted in the LECO 628CN Elementary Analyzer, to quantify the TOC contents.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify TOC using LECO 628CN Elementary Analyzer

steps.description: Determinations of the total sulfur contents were performed on the LECO 628S Elementary Analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine total sulfur content using LECO 628S

steps.description: Analysis in the Rock-Eval 6 equipment, 100 mg aliquots of the milled samples were sieved at 80 mesh.

methods.description: Gas chromatography analysis of organic extracts from shale samples.

steps.description: Approximately 50 g of sample was used in the ASE system, programmed to operate at a temperature of 150°C, at a pressure of 1,500 psi, performing three 15-minute wash cycles for each cycle, with 150 mL of dichloromethane per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: extract organic compounds using ASE system with dichloromethane

steps.description: Extracts were concentrated in a rotary evaporator and analyzed in a gas chromatograph and Agilent 7890B flame ionization detector (FID).
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate extracts and analyze using gas chromatography

methods.description: Analysis of saturated biomarkers using gas chromatography coupled to mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Organic extracts were fractionated by liquid chromatography. Saturated compounds were obtained using n-hexane.
steps.searchable_summary: fractionate extracts and obtain saturated compounds using n-hexane

steps.description: Saturated biomarkers were quantified by gas chromatography coupled to mass spectrometry (GC/MS) on Agilent model 7820A equipment.

methods.description: Determination of isotopic ratios δ 13 C and δ 18 O using Isotope Reason Mass Spectrometry (IRMS).

steps.description: Aliquots were used with approximately 50 mg of the decarbonated samples, which were then adequately packed in tin capsules and analyzed in IRMS system.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ 13 C ratio using IRMS

steps.description: Determinations of isotopic ratios δ 13 C and δ 18 O of the inorganic fraction were also made by IRMS.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ 18 O ratio using IRMS

methods.description: Characterization of palynofacies through isolation of organic content and slide preparation.

steps.description: Isolation of the organic content of the samples was done through successive acidifications to remove carbonic and siliciclastic components using HCl and HF.
To assemble the slides, a drop of the isolated kerogen, dilute acacia gum solution and a drop of distilled water was used on the cover slip.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides with isolated kerogen and acacia gum

steps.description: The evaluation and analysis of the slides were performed using Zeiss microscope model A2m.
